svg.text: MODERN HEBREW
SCRIPT — REVIVAL AND COEXISTENCE
MODERN HEBREW SCRIPT
Same square Assyrian block script as biblical period
Modern Israeli cursive (ktav yad): connected semi-cursive for handwriting
Print: block letters • Digital: same fonts as print • Handwriting: cursive
Normally unpointed (no nikud) except: children's books, poetry, dictionaries
BEN-YEHUDA'S CONTRIBUTION
Eliezer Ben-Yehuda (1858-1922): first Hebrew-only household in Jerusalem
Created thousands of new Hebrew words: milon (dictionary), iton (newspaper)
Academy of the Hebrew Language (1953): continues creating new terms
makhshev "computer" (from hashav "to think/calculate")
MODERN HEBREW DIGITAL SCRIPT
Unicode block: U+0590-U+05FF (Hebrew) includes all vowel marks
Right-to-left text: handled by Unicode BiDi algorithm
Mixed Hebrew-English text: common in modern Israeli writing
ARABIC · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
ARABIC IN MODERN ISRAEL
Arabic: official language of Israel alongside Hebrew (until 2018 downgrade)
2018 Nation-State Law: Hebrew = national language; Arabic = "special status"
~20% of Israeli citizens are Arab; Arabic-speaking communities throughout
BILINGUAL SIGNAGE AND COEXISTENCE
Israeli road signs: Hebrew + Arabic + English (trilingual)
Arabic Unicode: U+0600-U+06FF; right-to-left (same direction as Hebrew)
Two right-to-left scripts side by side on everyday Israeli street signs
ARABIC LOANWORDS IN MODERN HEBREW
ахlan "welcome" (from Arabic ahlan) • yalla "let's go" (Arabic)
wadi "dry riverbed" • balagan "mess" (via Arabic from Persian)
sababa "great/cool" • freier "sucker" (Yiddish but via Arabic context)
Modern Hebrew spoken: permeated with Arabic influence from Israeli Arabs
PLATE 12a ALT · MODERN HEBREW / ARABIC · SCRIPT
